message:
Revert place() alignment from round() to floor() for Go parity

Return to using floor() for alignment calculations in place() to match
the exact behavior of the original Go lipgloss library. While round()
appeared to give better visual results in some cases, floor() ensures
character-for-character output compatibility with Go lipgloss.

Go lipgloss uses integer division truncation (floor behavior) for
alignment padding. Matching this exactly is important for ports
that need to produce identical output.

Reverts the round() change from bd-3vqi.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
